projects.title: d8
projects.description: Created as part of the 2018 DigitalCrafts Immersive Coding Bootcamp. d8 is a web application that receives user input via a quiz. It outputs restaurant and movie suggestions in the user's area. I worked as a designer as part of a four-person team. I first prototyped the design using Adobe XD and then implemented the styles using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

projects.title: Blackjack
projects.description: A simple game of cards. Blackjack card game built with jQuery, Bootstrap, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.

projects.title: Hoot Hoot
projects.description: Working with input from the Rice University Study Abroad team, I designed a logo for multi-purpose use. It has appeared on the department's handouts, fans, and t-shirts.
add-credit: Rice University Study Abroad Logos courtesy Rice University Study Abroad.

projects.title: A Television Experience
projects.description: The ATX Television Festival's fifth season embraced the theme of "A Television Experience". With this as a starting point, I designed a series of materials for the festival. This included an advertisement that appeared in Entertainment Weekly.
add-credit: Photo and logo courtesy ATX Television Festival.

projects.title: TV Fan
projects.description: A series of designs working around the festival's fourth season theme, "As Close to Heaven as a Fan Can Get". I designed the festival poster and program guide cover to after what I imagine would be a TV fan's favorite wall art.
add-credit: Photo and logo courtesy ATX Television Festival.

projects.title: TV Camp for Grown Ups
projects.description: Camp-inspired gear and designs modeled around the festival's new tagline, "TV Camp for Grown Ups". Includes a full-page advertisement featured in Austin Monthly and Entertainment Weekly.
add-credit: Photo and logo courtesy ATX Television Festival.
